title: Trichipteris nigripes
intro: Trichipteris nigripes, commonly known as Black Lip Fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Trichipteris nigripes include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Black Lip Fern is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to take advantage of its potential health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.

It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Within the class Polypodiopsida, Trichipteris nigripes is a member of the subclass Polypodiidae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other fern species.
